articles.title: Cory Sandhagen says loss to Aljamain Sterling was TOUGHEST MOMENT of his UFC career | Daniel Cormier
articles.description: Daniel Cormier is joined by UFC Bantamweight Cory Sandhagen, where he talks about fighting Aljamain Sterling in a title eliminator fight back in 2020. Cory lost in the first round, and he talks about how that was the toughest moment of his career as it was his first loss in the UFC, and first time ever being finished. Cory was within an arms reach of UFC Gold, and was dominated by Aljo. Cory talks about how that fight helped him mentally for the rest of his career, and how to move forward after a loss. articles.title: Hey Jon Jones, let’s play WOULD YOU RATHER.
articles.description: Did Jon Jones really turn down $15 million… or did he make a much bigger mistake? Chael Sonnen breaks down the real story behind Jon Jones’ decision and why this isn’t about money—it’s about risk, control, and a misunderstanding that even smart fighters fall into. Using a simple “Would You Rather” scenario, Chael explains the difference between guaranteed money and chasing a percentage of something that may never materialize. It’s a lesson that applies far beyond fighting—and one that could define the final chapter of Jon Jones’ career. From the rumored White House fight to the risks of waiting for the “perfect deal,” Chael lays out why walking away from a sure thing can cost more than just one payday. This isn’t about Jon taking less. It’s about Jon choosing nothing. #UFC #JonJones #ChaelSonnen #MMA 0:28 $1M vs 80% Explained 1:03 Why Emotion Kills Logic 2:02 Risk = Reward 2:21 Eddie Hearn’s 80% Model 3:04 The Hidden Risk of Percentages 3:57 Jon Jones & $15M Offer 4:26 Why Opponent Matters 5:07 Jones Chooses Release 5:27 He Didn’t Take Less—He Took Zero 5:38 The Andre Ward Example 6:43 Time Is the Real Cost 7:02 The $18M Missed Opportunity 7:34 Final Question: Risk or Guarantee Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Tsarukyan’s Brutal Admission #ufc
articles.description: I just spent the day with Arman Tsarukyan — and what he told me caught me off guard. No excuses. No blame. No spin. When I asked about the incident with his opponent, I expected the usual answer — “he started it,” “something was said,” “things got heated.” Instead? Arman shut it down immediately. “He did nothing.” No trash talk. No cheap shot. No trigger. Then he went further. He said it was his plan all along. Wait until the match ended… and then strike. A punch. A knee to the body. Planned. That level of honesty is rare in fight sports. Most guys rewrite the story. They protect themselves. They shift the narrative. Arman didn’t. He owned it completely. And whether you agree with it or not, that kind of transparency forces a different conversation: Is it gamesmanship? Is it crossing a line? Or is it just raw competitiveness taken too far? Because in a sport built on intensity, emotion, and control, that moment sits right on the edge of all three. Arman didn’t justify it. He didn’t deny it. He told it exactly how it happened. And now it’s on everyone else to decide what it means. Respect the honesty — or question the action. But either way, the story just changed. #UFC #MMA #ArmanTsarukyan #FightNews #CombatSports #Wrestling #MMADrama #FightTalk #AthleteMindset

articles.title: McGregor Return Just Got Real #ufc
articles.description: I’m telling you — Conor McGregor is coming back. And not someday… soon. For a long time, the plan looked simple: Conor vs Michael Chandler. But here’s the problem — time. Over two years have passed. And when that much time goes by, you don’t just pick up where you left off. You reset. You go back to step one. That’s where things get complicated. Chandler still makes sense on paper — action, name value, guaranteed fireworks. But there’s a real question now: how much does he have left? How many miles are on that engine? So the conversation opens up. A fresh matchup like Ian Machado Garry starts gaining traction. New blood. Clean timing. A striking-heavy fight that tests Conor without throwing him straight into chaos. Other options? Less likely. Title fights? Doesn’t seem realistic. Big jumps in weight? Probably not happening. Legacy fights outside the division? Not right now. This is about getting Conor back in the cage — and doing it the right way. Because history tells us: You don’t return a superstar after years away and throw him into the deep end immediately. You build it. Step by step. But make no mistake — the return is real. The opponent? That’s the question. And the clock is ticking. #UFC #MMA #ConorMcGregor #MichaelChandler #IanGarry #UFCReturn #FightNews #CombatSports #MMARumors

articles.title: Eddie Hearn Is Fighting the Wrong Battle
articles.description: Chael Sonnen explains why Eddie Hearn entering MMA is a losing strategy and why the real battle isn’t where he thinks it is. Using examples like Mark Cuban stepping back after exploring MMA promotion and the dominance of Dana White, Chael breaks down what actually makes a fight promotion work, and why simply having talent or resources isn’t enough. From the lack of structure in combat sports to the realities of building a roster and running events consistently, this is a deeper look at why MMA isn’t as easy to enter as it seems. The message is clear: if Eddie wants to win, he needs to fight Dana in boxing, not follow him into MMA. #ufc #EddieHearn #DanaWhite #MMA #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Eddie Hearn Enters MMA 0:20 Chael’s Warning 0:50 The Mark Cuban Lesson 1:30 Why He Walked Away 2:00 The Real Mistake 2:30 Dana vs Eddie Explained 3:00 Why MMA Isn’t Easy 3:30 One Event vs Many 4:00 The Roster Problem 4:30 “Best Fighters” Myth 5:00 No Real Structure 5:30 The Media Game 6:00 Where Eddie Loses 6:30 The Fight He Should Take 6:50 Final Verdict Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: UFC Didn’t Need Jon Jones?s #ufc
articles.description: This is where things get interesting. Jon Jones says he was ready. Willing. Able. Even willing to take less money — around $15 million — to make the fight happen. And still… it didn’t happen. According to Jones, he was prepared to face Alex Pereira instead of waiting on Tom Aspinall. Different opponent, different path — same goal: get back in the cage. But the UFC didn’t go that direction. Now you’ve got two versions of the story: Jones says he was in. The UFC says otherwise. Somebody’s not lining up with reality. But here’s the part that actually matters: The UFC moved on without him. That’s the headline. Because for years, Jon Jones was the center of everything — the biggest name, the biggest draw, the guy you had to build around. Now? The machine kept going. New stars. New matchups. New plans. And no Jones required. That doesn’t mean he’s not great. It doesn’t mean he’s not still dangerous. It means the promotion proved something: No one is bigger than the schedule. Fights will happen. Belts will move. Cards will get built. With or without you. That’s the reality of the fight business. And for the first time, Jon Jones might be on the outside looking in. #UFC #MMA #JonJones #AlexPereira #TomAspinall #DanaWhite #FightNews #MMABusiness #CombatSports

articles.title: Dana’s “Slip” Changes Everything #ufc
articles.description: Did Dana White just let it slip? There’s growing belief that Conor McGregor vs Ian Machado Garry is being lined up — and not everyone thinks it was an accident. The clue? Dana talking about returning to Dublin… but making it clear that Conor vs Garry wouldn’t happen in Ireland. That’s not a denial. That’s a redirect. Some are calling it a Freudian slip — the kind where the truth sneaks out before it’s supposed to. Because if the fight wasn’t real, why even separate the location from the matchup? And stylistically? It’s fascinating. Two strikers. Two personalities. Two very different stages of their careers. Garry has openly shown admiration for McGregor. There’s respect there — but also opportunity. A win over Conor changes everything for a rising contender. For McGregor, it’s a different equation: Return fight. Timing test. Sharp, technical opponent. No easy tune-up. This wouldn’t be nostalgia. This would be real risk. And that’s what makes it compelling. The UFC loves matchups that blend star power with stakes — and this checks both boxes. Nothing official yet. But sometimes, the truth shows itself early. And if this fight does get announced? It might’ve already been revealed. #UFC #MMA #ConorMcGregor #IanGarry #DanaWhite #FightNews #MMARumors #CombatSports #UFCReturn

articles.title: Joe Rogan Called the White House Card a Gimmick
articles.description: Get $10 Off at BRUNT with code CHAEL at https://www.bruntworkwear.com/CHAEL #BruntpodChaelChael Sonnen responds to Joe Rogan calling the White House card a “gimmick” and breaks down why that framing misses the bigger picture. From potential matchups like Alex Pereira moving up to heavyweight to face Ciryl Gane, to Ilia Topuria vs Justin Gaethje, Chael explains why the actual fights—and the stakes behind them—are what matter most. He dives into whether title fights truly elevate a card, why expectations may have gotten out of control, and how real storylines often get overlooked when fans focus on presentation instead of competition. articles.title: Israel Adesanya Thinks He Was Underappreciated, He Wasn’t...
articles.description: Chael Sonnen reacts to Israel Adesanya saying he may have been underappreciated—and explains why that’s only partially true. While some fans may have overlooked just how difficult his run was, Chael breaks down the reality of Izzy’s career: calling his own shots, taking on dangerous matchups like Yoel Romero, Anderson Silva, and Alex Pereira, and even moving up in weight to chase greatness. From controlling his own path as champion to consistently taking risks others wouldn’t, Adesanya built one of the most unique runs in UFC history. The question isn’t whether he was appreciated—it’s whether fans truly understood what they were watching in real time. #UFC #IsraelAdesanya #MMA #AlexPereira #ChaelSonnen Timestamps 0:00 “Izzy Was Underappreciated?” 0:30 Chael Pushes Back 1:00 The Chimaev Problem 1:40 What We Loved About Him 2:10 Izzy’s Real Advantage 2:50 He Controlled Everything 3:20 The Romero Risk 3:50 Facing His Idol 4:20 Pereira Was Different 4:50 The Move to 205 5:20 The Jon Jones Plan 5:50 Nobody Had It Harder 6:20 Fans Have Short Memories 6:50 Final Truth on Izzy Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Jake Paul Is Playing a Dangerous Game
articles.description: Chael Sonnen reacts to Jake Paul claiming that Francis Ngannou can’t box—and breaks down why that statement doesn’t add up. After watching Ngannou push Tyson Fury to the limit and nearly walk away with the heavyweight title, Chael questions the narrative while also giving Jake full credit for proving his toughness against names like Nate Diaz and Anderson Silva. But does Jake really need to take on Ngannou, or has he already proven everything he set out to prove? articles.title: The Real Reason Conor vs Chandler Isn’t Happening
articles.description: Chael Sonnen is confident that Conor McGregor is officially on his way back—but not in the way fans expected. After direct conversations and behind-the-scenes movement, Chael explains why the long-awaited fight with Michael Chandler is no longer the right matchup and what the UFC’s real plan is for Conor’s return. From the impact of his injury to the importance of rebuilding momentum, Chael lays out how this comeback will be structured and why Conor may be thrown directly into high-level competition against top contenders like Ian Machado Garry or others in the division. This isn’t about hype—it’s about proving he still belongs. The real question isn’t if Conor returns… it’s what happens when he does. #UFC #ConorMcGregor #MichaelChandler #MMA #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Conor McGregor Is Coming Back 0:30 Why Chael Is Certain 1:00 Why Chandler Fight Is Dead 1:40 UFC’s Brand Promise Explained 2:20 Why They Have to Restart 3:00 Why Chandler No Longer Fits 3:40 Who Conor Could Fight Instead 4:20 The Anderson Silva Comparison 5:10 How the UFC Will Use Conor 5:50 Conor Is Willing to Fight Anyone 6:30 The Weight Class Surprise 7:00 Why This Comeback Is Different 7:40 The Real Stakes of This Fight 8:10 One Fight Decides Everything Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Netflix Isn’t Beating the UFC, It’s Proving Them Right
articles.description: Netflix Isn’t Beating the UFC, It’s Proving Them Right Chael Sonnen breaks down why the idea that Netflix can compete with the UFC is completely misguided. While big names like Ronda Rousey, Francis Ngannou, and Nate Diaz bring attention, Chael explains why stacking former stars doesn’t build a real promotion. From sold-out UFC arenas without announced main events to the deeper reality of roster building, rankings, and star creation, this is a sharp look at why the UFC remains untouchable. Chael makes the case that Netflix isn’t threatening the UFC—it’s reinforcing exactly why the UFC model works. For more analysis and inside perspective, stay locked in. #UFC #MMA #Netflix #DanaWhite #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Netflix Is Proving the UFC Right 0:20 Why Fans Don’t Know What They Want 0:50 The “Best Fighters” Argument 1:20 Big Names vs Real Competition 1:50 Why This Isn’t a Real Promotion 2:10 Netflix Is Buying Memories 2:40 The Ronda Rousey Angle 3:10 Fighter Pay vs Real Threat 3:40 The Perth Sellout Example 4:10 The Difference Between UFC and Everyone Else 4:40 Why You Can’t Beat the UFC 5:10 The Real Problem: Building Stars 5:40 Gaethje & Chandler Example 6:10 The Power of “UFC” 6:40 Final Reality Check Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: The Team Race Myth in College Wrestling #ufc
articles.description: Let’s tell the truth nobody wants to say: Wrestlers don’t care about the team race. Not because they’re selfish. Not because they’re bad teammates. Because wrestling is the ultimate individual sport. When you step on that mat, it’s just you. Your lungs. Your weight cut. Your mistakes. Your moment. Scoreboards don’t shoot takedowns. Team points don’t fight off your back. The “team title” conversation? That’s for coaching staffs, media desks, and broadcast graphics. Ask the athlete cutting weight at 6 AM. Ask the guy icing his knees alone in the hallway. Ask the wrestler staring at the bracket with one name circled. He’s chasing a national title. His title. And dominance doesn’t ruin the sport. Programs like Penn State Nittany Lions wrestling don’t hurt wrestling — they raise the standard. Dynasties don’t make competition worse. They force everyone else to level up. Better rooms. Better systems. Better athletes. Greatness creates pressure. Pressure creates growth. The sport doesn’t suffer when one program wins. The sport evolves. Because wrestling has always been simple: One mat. One match. One man’s will. Team trophies are celebrated. Individual titles are remembered. And every wrestler knows the difference. Win your match. Score your points. Earn your legacy. Everything else is noise. #Wrestling #CollegeWrestling #NCAAWrestling #PennStateWrestling #CombatSports #Grappling #MatLife #WrestlerMindset #IndividualSport

articles.title: The Legend of Chuck Norris #ufc
articles.description: “Feel bad for the Grim Reaper having to face Chuck Norris.” That joke exists for a reason. Chuck Norris wasn’t just a movie tough guy. He was a legitimate, seven-time world champion who earned his status the hard way — through discipline, competition, and respect for the craft. And what made him different? Humility. He never chased fantasy matchups. Never claimed he could beat heavyweight boxers. Never tried to steal shine. While others argued about who they could defeat, Norris focused on what he could learn. He openly respected greats like Mike Tyson, recognizing elite skill when he saw it. No ego. No noise. Just a martial artist who understood mastery takes different forms. That mindset carried him beyond competition. When he transitioned into film and television, Norris didn’t just build a career — he helped make martial arts mainstream. Generations were introduced to discipline, honor, and combat sports through his presence on screen. He made toughness look controlled. He made confidence look quiet. He made martial arts look cool. Most legends leave highlights. Few leave reputations. Norris walked away with both. In a fight world full of hype and callouts, his legacy stands on something rarer: credibility without arrogance. Win with class. Compete with respect. Leave the sport better than you found it. That’s why the jokes live on. That’s why the respect never fades. #ChuckNorris #MartialArts #CombatSports #Legends #FightCulture #Respect #MartialArtsMovies #CombatLegacy

articles.title: Did Dana Accidentally Reveal Conor’s Opponent?
articles.description: Chael Sonnen reacts to what may have been an accidental announcement from Dana White—did he just reveal Conor McGregor’s next opponent? After watching the interview closely, Chael believes Dana may have let it slip: Conor McGregor vs. Ian Garry. While it wasn’t framed as an official announcement, the wording—and the context—suggest something more than a simple mistake. Chael breaks down why this fight actually makes sense. Conor has made it clear he wants to compete at 170 pounds and isn’t interested in picking favorable matchups. Meanwhile, Ian Garry has been waiting for a big opportunity after previously accepting a high-risk fight that never materialized. There’s also the added layer of respect between the two fighters, making this a unique and potentially complicated matchup. Was this a true announcement… or a rare slip from Dana White? #UFC #ConorMcGregor #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Did Dana Announce It? 0:25 Conor Wants 170 0:53 The Prates Fight Story 1:14 Why Garry Is a Tough Matchup 1:45 The “Slip” Explained 2:13 Why This Fight Makes Sense 2:45 Conor’s Mindset 3:45 Why Garry Deserves It 4:13 Final Thoughts on the Fight Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: JIRI PROCHAZKA WANTS A BMF BELT + UFC TITLE! | UFC 327 EXCLUSIVE INTERVIEW
articles.description: Michael Bisping and Paul Felder speak with Jiri Prochazka ahead of his return for the light heavyweight title, as he discusses a potential BMF belt at 205, his thoughts on Max Holloway vs Charles Oliveira, and his upcoming fight against Carlos Ulberg. Jiri also opens up on becoming a father just days after the fight, how it’s changed his mindset, and why this is a new, more focused version of himself heading into Miami. articles.title: Dana White Reacts to Nate Diaz Leaving UFC 😳
articles.description: 🚨 Dana White finally addressed Nate Diaz’s situation… After talks of a UFC comeback 👀 👉 Diaz chose to fight on the MVP/Netflix card instead And Dana’s response? “I think Nate got an offer he couldn’t refuse.” 😳💰 He confirmed: • They actually met about a return • But Diaz took the bigger opportunity elsewhere 👉 “I’m happy for him.” 💭 Did the UFC miss out… or did Nate make the right move? 👇 What do you think?

articles.title: I Thought No One Would Show Up… 😂 | BISPING RUNS in LONDON! 🏃
articles.description: Michael Bisping meets fans for a casual run in London ahead of UFC London… but things get out of hand fast. What was supposed to be a small meet-up turns into a HUGE turnout, with hundreds of fans joining Bisping for a 3+ mile run through the streets. Along the way, Bisping answers questions, talks UFC London, gives predictions, and shares a few laughs with fans. Plus appearances from UFC fighters, a surprise supplement truck, and one of the best atmospheres you’ll see outside the Octagon. 🔒 Become a Member for special Perks, Exclusive Content and ultra-exclusive Live Chats: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCDrG2_1TcVkXKXXsD6Kjwig/join #bisping #ufc #mma #mixedmartialarts #michaelbisping #running #run #UFCLondon #london #fitness
